subject: english
topic_title: Argumentative Essay: The Home Is Responsible for the Unruly Behaviour of Teenagers in the Society

sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: An argumentative essay presents a subject with the aim of persuading the reader to agree with one point of view against another.

sections.heading: Features
sections.content: An argumentative essay requires the writer to present a subject with a view to persuading the reader to accept one side of an issue. The essence of this type of essay is argument, and its subject matter often covers forms of debate. Not all argumentative essays are debates. An argument may be developed to prove a point, present a viewpoint, or balance the two sides of an issue.

sections.heading: Debates vs Essays
sections.content: Debates are framed with audience recognition and greetings, while essays present viewpoints without directly addressing an audience.

sections.heading: Example
sections.content: Write an essay presenting your points for or against the topic: 'Day students perform better than boarding students in examinations'.
